SEARCH
Package controller

Class CompareController

java.lang.Object
controller.CompareController
public class CompareController extends Object
Controls the side-by-side comparison of two sorting algorithms. Manages data generation, starting/pausing/resetting the sort, and notifies the view when both sorts are done.

  • Constructor Summary

  • Constructor Details

    • CompareController

      public CompareController(CompareWindow view, String algo1, String algo2, String genType, double entropy, int initialSize)
      Creates a new CompareController object.
      Parameters:
      view - the comparison window this controller manages
      algo1 - name of the first algorithm
      algo2 - name of the second algorithm
      genType - type of input generator
      entropy - entropy level for the input
      initialSize - initial size of the arrays

  • Method Details

    • generateData

      public void generateData()
      Generates a fresh pair of identical lists and updates the view. Both algorithms get the same data so the comparison is fair.

    • start

      public void start()
      Starts the sort, or resumes it if it was paused. Wires up both visualization listeners and kicks off both sort runners in parallel.

    • pause

      public void pause()
      Pauses both sorts if they are currently running. Does nothing if already paused or not running.

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Cancels any running sorts, resets all state, and generates fresh data. Also resumes listeners before cancelling to avoid them getting stuck.

    • onOneSortDone

      public void onOneSortDone()
      Called by each sort runner when it finishes. Once both have finished, it notifies the view to show the final results. Synchronized because both runners call this from separate threads.

    • setArraySize

      public void setArraySize(int size)
      Updates the array size and regenerates data if no sort is running.
      Parameters:
      size - the new array size

    • getStats1

      public SortStats getStats1()
      Returns:
      the stats collected for the first algorithm

    • getStats2

      public SortStats getStats2()
      Returns:
      the stats collected for the second algorithm
